steps.title: Understand what a point on the equation means
steps.content: If a point $(x, y)$ satisfies $y = f(x)$, then by definition, the $y$-value at that point equals $f$ evaluated at the $x$-value. In other words, $f(x) = y$.

steps.title: Apply the definition to the given point
steps.content: The ordered pair is $(6, 4)$. The $x$-coordinate is 6 and the $y$-coordinate is 4. Therefore, $f(6) = 4$. A is correct.
